rulings.Case Title: IN RE: HSH HOLDINGS LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petitioner Hsh Holdings Llc'S Verified Petition To Release Property From Claim Of Mechanic'S Lien Of Respondent Arcon Construction Corporation
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 9, 2024 line 1. HEARING ON PETITIONER HSH HOLDINGS LLC'S VERIFIED PETITION TO RELEASE PROPERTY FROM CLAIM OF MECHANIC'S LIEN OF RESPONDENT ARCON CONSTRUCTION CORPORATION is OFF CALENDAR, per the request of moving party. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: RIDGEGATE APARTMENTS, INC. ET AL VS. AMERICAN WEST PARKING SERVICES, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Renewed Motion For Judgment Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 664.6
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 9, 2024 line 5. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 664.6 is GRANTED, in part. Plaintiff properly filed the stipulation for entry of judgment as a separate document with first appearance fee paid for Defendant, so Defendant is now a "party" within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure Section 664.6 such that the Court has jurisdiction to enforce the parties' settlement agreement, and hereby enforces the agreement as follows: Plaintiff shall have judgment against Defendant American West Parking Services, Inc., in the amount of $501,515.16 in unpaid rent. There is insufficient evidence to support the late charges. Plaintiff is awarded reasonable attorney fees in the amount of $20,681.823. The net amount to which Plaintiff is entitled under this judgment shall bear interest at the rate of 10 percent per annum or such other rate as the Legislature may declare applicable in according with Code of Civil Procedure section 685.010, from the date of entry of this judgment until the date it is satisfied. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: THE 300 COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA GENERAL PARTNERSHIP VS. DIGITIAL REVOLUTION MEDIA CENTER LLC A CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 9, 2024 line 10. PLAINTIFF THE 300 COMPANY'S APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER is DENIED without prejudice. Moving party seeks to attach past due rent, holdover damages, CAM charges, estimated costs and attorney's fees. There is no evidence to support the costs and fees request. rulings.Case Title: RYAN DRUM VS. WILLIAM H. TEALER
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 9, 2024 line 11. DEFENDANT KENNETH LITTLE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. Plaintiff sufficiently states a cause of action for unlawful detainer under California Code of Civil Procedure, subsection 1161a. Under CCP Section 430.10(c), Defendant's civil case for wrongful foreclosure is not the same cause of action against Plaintiff's unlawful detainer action. (Bush v. Superior Court (1992) 10 Cal.App.4th 1374.) =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: 1201 TENNESSEE LP VS. BONNIE JONES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 9, 2024 line 15. DEFENDANTS BONNIE JONES AND BAUDILLO FUENTES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. Plaintiff's Complaint pleads that it served Defendants with a 3-Day Notice to Cure or Quit with the Form 1007 issued by the San Francisco Rent Board issued August 11, 2021. The Court takes judicial notice of the Form 1007 issued by the San Francisco Rent Board on July 28, 2023. Under Section 37.9(c) of the San Francisco Rent Ordinance, a landlord shall attach a copy of "the form" that the San Francisco Rent Board prepared, which is the current version of the form. Thus, the Complaint fails to state that Plaintiff attached the San Francisco Rent Board's Form 1007 to the 3-Day Notice to Cure or Quit. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
